concentration.headline: Moderately concentrated — one leading city
concentration.body: Tokyo leads Japan's urban system with 20.4% of tracked urban population across 1344 cities — significant but not overwhelming. Secondary cities retain meaningful economic weight alongside the capital.
concentration.comparison: Similar to: United Kingdom, Italy, Canada
concentration.contrast: Unlike: USA, Japan (more distributed) or Thailand, Argentina (more dominant)

gravity.headline: Dense gravitational cluster — Tokyo & Yokohama
gravity.body: The strongest gravitational pull exists between Tokyo and Yokohama (29 km apart, combined population 41,542,630). Cities form a tight gravitational cluster with top pairs averaging 22 km apart. This dense sub-regional network often functions as a single megalopolitan economy.

narration.synthesis: Japan sits between distributed and dominant: Tokyo leads with 20.4% but doesn't overwhelm. Gravity clusters tightly around Tokyo → Yokohama (22 km avg) — a strong sub-regional core coexists with the national leader, creating a layered urban hierarchy.
